member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.DatasourcesRaster.IAddNewerRastersParameters
member.summary: Provides access to members that control parameters for adding newer rasters to a mosaic dataset.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.DatasourcesRaster.IAddNewerRastersParameters.OverwriteExisting
member.summary: Indicates whether existing mosaic dataset items are updated with datasets that have been modified on disk. When set to false, the operation skips over modified data source items that already exist in the mosaic dataset.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.DatasourcesRaster.IAddRastersParameters
member.summary: Provides access to members that control parameters for adding rasters to a mosaic dataset.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.DatasourcesRaster.IAddRastersParameters.AllowInPlaceSync
member.summary: Inidicates whether to allow items to be build as soon as they are added to the mosaic dataset (as long as the raster type allows in-place synchronization).

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.DatasourcesRaster.IAddRastersParameters.Crawler
member.summary: The data source crawler that provides incoming data source items.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.DatasourcesRaster.IAddRastersParameters.DuplicateItemsAction
member.summary: Indicates how incoming duplicates items are handled. Item URIs with the same key value are assumed to be duplicate.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.DatasourcesRaster.IAddRastersParameters.Filter
member.summary: Regular expression formed using wild card characters ('*', '?', or '.') for matching data source items created by the crawler. No item URIs are requested for those data sources don't match the specified regular expression.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.DatasourcesRaster.IAddRastersParameters.RasterType
member.summary: The raster type used for constructing raster datasets using incoming data source items.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.DatasourcesRaster.IAddRastersParameters.SynchronizeItems
member.summary: Indicates whether catalog items are synchronized once added to the mosaic dataset.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.DatasourcesRaster.IAddRastersParameters.TimeExtent
member.summary: The range of allowed time value for Item URIs. Item URIs with 'SourceTimeStamp' that don't lie within the specified range are filtered out.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.DatasourcesRaster.IRaster2.RasterDataset
member.summary: The parent raster dataset.
member.remarks:
The RasterDataset object represents a dataset on disk or in a database. It is composed of one or more persistent raster bands. The dataset provides access only to operations that apply to the entire dataset, with the RasterBand providing access to operations that occur on individual bands.

The RasterDataset object performs basic dataset management functions, such as copy, rename, and delete. It can also instantiat RasterBand and Raster objects representing other aspects of the data. It can be used to examine dataset properties including different format, extent, spatial reference, and number of bands.

The spatial reference of the raster raster dataset can be changed through interface IGeoDatasetSchemaEdit, but it does not project the dataset, only changes the coordinate system associated with the dataset.
The pyramid of the raster dataset can be built or rebuilt to improve display performance for large raster datasets.

The RasterDataset object performs basic dataset management functions, such as copy, rename, and delete. It can also instantiat RasterBand and Raster objects representing other aspects of the data. The pyramid of the raster dataset can be built or rebuilt to improve display performance for large raster datasets.

member.name: P:ESRI.ArcGIS.DatasourcesRaster.IRasterBand.RasterDataset
member.summary: The RasterDataset associated with this raster band.
member.remarks:
The RasterDataset object represents a dataset on disk or in a database. It is composed of one or more persistent raster bands. The dataset provides access only to operations that apply to the entire dataset, with the RasterBand providing access to operations that occur on individual bands.

The RasterDataset object performs basic dataset management functions, such as copy, rename, and delete. It can also instantiat RasterBand and Raster objects representing other aspects of the data. It can be used to examine dataset properties including different format, extent, spatial reference, and number of bands.

The spatial reference of the raster raster dataset can be changed through interface IGeoDatasetSchemaEdit, but it does not project the dataset, only changes the coordinate system associated with the dataset.
The pyramid of the raster dataset can be built or rebuilt to improve display performance for large raster datasets.

member.summary: The raster dataset to crawl.
member.remarks:
The RasterDataset object represents a dataset on disk or in a database. It is composed of one or more persistent raster bands. The dataset provides access only to operations that apply to the entire dataset, with the RasterBand providing access to operations that occur on individual bands.

The RasterDataset object performs basic dataset management functions, such as copy, rename, and delete. It can also instantiat RasterBand and Raster objects representing other aspects of the data. It can be used to examine dataset properties including different format, extent, spatial reference, and number of bands.

The spatial reference of the raster raster dataset can be changed through interface IGeoDatasetSchemaEdit, but it does not project the dataset, only changes the coordinate system associated with the dataset.
The pyramid of the raster dataset can be built or rebuilt to improve display performance for large raster datasets.

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.DatasourcesRaster.IRasterTypeDataSourceHandler
member.summary: Provides access to members that control the raster type data source handler.

Indicates whether a valid raster type is available in the specified collection that's capable of opening the given data source and returns a collection of all such valid raster types. This method uses all sensor raster types if IRasterTypeDataSourceHandlerPara

Builds the specified data source using the specified raster types and returns the constructed function raster dataset. This method uses all sensor raster types if no input raster type array is supplied. The method will set IRasterTypeDataSourceHandlerParameter

Searches the given folder path, non-recursively, for files that can be opened by the specified collection of raster types and returns an array of valid file paths. This method uses all sensor raster types if IRasterTypeDataSourceHandlerParameters.AllowBuilderI

member.name: T:ESRI.ArcGIS.DatasourcesRaster.IRasterTypeDataSourceHandler2
member.summary: Provides access to members that control the raster type data source handler.

Opens the specified data source as a raster dataset or builds it using the specified raster types and returns the set of constructed IRasterDataset and/or IBuilderItem objects depending on the values of IRasterTypeDataSourceHandlerParameters.AllowRasterDataset
